artifacts.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
assertions: Cervical radiculopathy referral assessment includes gait disturbance, clumsy or weak hands or legs, brisk reflexes, extensor plantar responses and new bladder or bowel disturbance.
Persistent disabling or uncontrolled pain and age under 20 modify referral decisions; rapidly progressive weakness and swallowing or breathing compromise require urgent assessment.

artifacts.title: ACR Appropriateness Criteria: Cervical Pain or Cervical Radiculopathy
assertions: Imaging pathways distinguish acute or increasing pain, radiculopathy, trauma, infection, malignancy and prior cervical surgery contexts.
The source supports collecting red flags and prior procedure context but is not used by runtime to order or diagnose.

artifacts.title: Meningococcal Disease Symptoms and Complications
assertions: Meningitis symptoms can include fever, headache, stiff neck, confusion, nausea or vomiting and photophobia.
Meningococcal disease can worsen rapidly and requires immediate medical attention.

artifacts.title: STOM neck-pain terminology and MRCM lookup
assertions: Build-time lookup confirmed active Neck pain (finding) 81680005, Cervical radiculopathy (disorder) 54404000 and Neck stiffness (finding) 161882006 with Korean descriptions.
MRCM lookup for Neck pain returned Severity 246112005 and Finding site 363698007 among allowed attributes; terminology does not determine urgency or diagnosis.
